violations.text: Observed soap not available at the hand-washing sink in the front of establishment. PIC refilled soap dispenser at time of insepction. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
Observed the hand-washing sink in the back of the establishment lacking paper towels. PIC restocked paper towel at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

Observed the hand-washing sink in the back of establishment blocked by mop bucket. PIC relocated mop bucket to designated area. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of (500) parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label (150-400 ppm). PIC diluted sanitizer and retested to (150-400 ppm). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

At the time of inspection, Utensil in the front service area were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Methods for proper storage of in-use utensils were reviewed at the time of inspection. PIC made correction at time of inspection and stored utensil in cup without water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
Observed 2 door refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the (back of establishment). New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026
Observed the following non-food contact surfaces of the: -Ice buckets -Prep unit cold drawer shelving has an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The floors underneath equipment in the front and back of establishment has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at with RIDOH CFP all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025
Observed True 2-door Refrigerator and Superior refrigerator gaskets were observed in poor repair. Gaskets shall be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. -Application provided to PIC New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Coffee and ice were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Proper hand-washing sink use was discussed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Turkey sausage was observed held at (47) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded turkey sausage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025
Observed flip top held open by a screw located in the sandwich station was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025
Observed True 2-door Refrigerator and Superior refrigerator gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025
The front hand wash sink was observed being used as a hand sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

An employees' beverage container (non-spill proof) was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

The following utensils were observed stored incorrectly: 1) A scoop was stored with its handle in direct contact with dry ingredients 2) Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

Food racks were observed stored in, 'pet station'. These racks were stored in a manner that did not protect from miscellaneous sources of contamination within this area. PIC instructed that racks must be behind partition and covered with disposable covers. PIC removed racks from this area at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

Employee was observed not changing gloves when switching tasks or leaving their station. If used, SINGLE-USE gloves shall be used for only one task such as working with READY-TO-EAT FOOD or with raw animal FOOD, used for no other purpose, and discarded when damaged or soiled, or when interruptions occur in the operation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Per PIC, all equipment/utensils needing to be sanitized will be transported to nearby facility (under same ownership) for ware-washing until unit is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

Several foods were observed held at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

A scoop for dry ingredients was stored with the handle in direct contact with the food product. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

The walk-in cooler was observed in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature). All TCS foods were moved to another refrigeration unit or voluntarily disposed. Walk-in cooler is not to be used to store TCS foods until service is provided and approval is given by RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
A mop was stored in stagnant water between uses. Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
